summary: DHH notes a Wired article observing that TV shows like 24, Felicity, and Alias consistently depict villains using Windows PCs while heroes use Macs. He connects this cultural signal to his own household, announcing he's converting one of his three Windows XP machines to FreeBSD for server use, aided by a FreeBSD-enthusiast friend who promises a 15-minute install. He acknowledges that developing PHP with Apache and MySQL was always a poor fit on Windows.
key_points: Television shows were already encoding Windows as the villain's platform and Macs as the hero's choice
DHH was preparing to migrate his server from Windows XP to FreeBSD, influenced by a zealot friend
Developing open-source web stacks (PHP, Apache, MySQL) on Windows was seen as a compromised experience
money_quotes: In Fox's hit TV show, 24, starring Kiefer Sutherland, the villains use PCs running Microsoft Windows. The good guys, of course, use Macs.
My household of three XP machines will loose one to the side of free software tomorrow.
Developing PHP for use on Apache and MySQL was always going to be second best on a Windows box.
key_insight: Even in 2002, the cultural and practical tide was turning against Windows, both in pop culture's villain-coding and in developers migrating to free software for real web development work.
